title: EHR Overlay — Phase B observations not present in CapabilityStatement
description: Per-EHR overlay file. The CapabilityStatement is the authoritative spec source (fetched verbatim from each EHR's sandbox, stored alongside this file). The overlay captures ONLY the things a CapabilityStatement cannot tell you: per-element value-set deviations observed in real responses, OperationOutcome shapes triggered in error paths, pagination behavior measured by walking results, auth flow specifics not in the security extension. Every overlay claim carries a citation + verified date + verification method. The pipeline reads CapabilityStatement (authoritative spec) + overlay (measured behavior) and synthesizes the Map at render time — no intermediate file.

ehr_version_validated.description: Specific EHR version measured (e.g., 'Epic February 2026'). Source: CapabilityStatement.software.version when present.

capability_statement_fetched_date.description: ISO date the sibling CapabilityStatement.json was fetched.

capability_statement_url.description: Live URL of the metadata endpoint the CapabilityStatement was fetched from.

compatibility_statement.description: Legal-safe compatibility claim. MUST avoid trademark; MUST disclaim affiliation.

auth_overlay.description: Auth specifics NOT captured in CapabilityStatement.rest.security. Things like JWT signing alg, JWKS hosting requirements, scope format version, client-registration mechanics.

operation_outcome_overlay.description: OperationOutcome shape observed when triggering errors. Not in CapabilityStatement.

trigger.description: What request triggered this OperationOutcome (HTTP verb + URL + headers)

pagination_overlay.description: Pagination behavior observed by walking real results. Not in CapabilityStatement. Numeric fields nullable until Phase B verification is complete.

element_deviations.description: Per-element observed deviations from US Core MUST-SUPPORT, OBSERVED in sandbox responses. Each entry is a Phase B finding the CapabilityStatement does not declare.

row_id.description: Stable 12-hex-char identifier derived deterministically from (profile_id, path, deviation_category). Citation anchor — external essays/blog posts link to this id, not the array index. See tools/row_id.py.

previous_row_ids.description: Prior row_ids this row replaced (e.g. when deviation_category was re-categorized). Used to 301-redirect stale external citations.

expected_per_us_core.description: Verbatim US Core requirement (allowed values, cardinality, format)

observed_in_ehr.description: Verbatim what the EHR actually returned

phase_b_findings.description: Editorial summary of the most consequential behaviors observed during Phase B. Populated by hand from sweep results; preserved across re-sweeps unless explicitly contradicted.

multi_patient_coverage.description: Per-(profile, path) summary of how many sandbox patients each element was tested against. Populated by tools.measure_phase_b multi-patient driver.

access_scope_notes.description: For vendors that publish more than one host front fronting the same backend (e.g., Oracle Health's fhir-ehr.cerner.com provider tier vs fhir-myrecord.cerner.com patient tier). Captures the deltas between scopes — hosts, resource subsets, SMART context differences — without forcing parallel ehrs/{ehr}-patient/ directories.

description.description: One-paragraph framing of the scopes this vendor publishes.

host_front.description: Base URL or hostname for this scope.

smart_context.description: Primary SMART launch context this scope advertises (e.g., context-ehr-encounter, context-standalone-patient).

resources_present.description: Resources advertised on this scope but not on every other scope. Empty = no scope-only resources.

search_param_observations.description: Per-resource search behavior verified beyond what CapStmt declares (e.g., silently-ignored params, value-set restrictions on token searches).

params_silently_ignored.description: Search params accepted by HTTP but with no filtering effect — the worst kind of bug, never declared in CapStmt

restriction.description: e.g., 'category only accepts [laboratory, vital-signs]; rejects social-history with OperationOutcome'

source_quote.description: Verbatim evidence excerpt (URL response body snippet, doc quote)

verified_query.description: If sandbox: full HTTP request that produced the evidence
